title: Custom Iterator Pattern
description: Implement the Iterator trait on your own types to make them work with for loops and all of Rust's iterator combinators (map, filter, collect, sum, etc.). Custom iterators are lazy and composable, enabling efficient data processing pipelines without intermediate allocations.
whenToUse: When your type represents a sequence of values
Lazy generation of data (Fibonacci, range, permutations)
Walking custom data structures (trees, graphs, linked lists)
When you want your type to integrate with the iterator ecosystem
realWorldUse: std::ops::Range (for i in 0..10), std::str::Chars, std::io::BufReader::lines(), std::fs::ReadDir. Most collection types implement IntoIterator. Regex::captures_iter, walkdir::WalkDir.
